en.extract:
The barred cuckoo-dove (Macropygia unchall) is a bird species in the family Columbidae. It is native to South and Southeast Asia, and listed as Least Concern on the IUCN Red List.


Taxonomy

German herpetologist Johann Georg Wagler first described the barred cuckoo-dove in 1827. It has three recognized subspecies:

M. u. tusalia (Blyth, 1843)
M. u. minor (Swinhoe, 1870)
M. u. unchall (Wagler, 1827)


Description

The barred cuckoo-dove has a buff coloured throat and forehead which becomes pinkish grey at the crown. measures 37 to 41 cm (15 to 16 in) in length, and weighs 153 to 182 g (5.4 to 6.4 oz).  Its iris is yellow or pale brown, the beak is black and short, and the feet are red. It has blackish brown upperparts. The back, mantle (between the nape and the starting of the back), rump, wing coverts, and scapulars have reddish brown fringes. The tail is blackish brown, and is heavily barred reddish brown.
It is similar to the little cuckoo-dove, but it is much larger and darker, and is black-barred on the mantle, breast, coverts, and tail.


Distribution and habitat
The barred cuckoo-dove occurs from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia. It inhabits dense subtropical woodlands at altitudes of 800 to 3,000 m (2,600 to 9,800 ft) from sea level, on montane slopes. It prefers clearings and edges of old-growth forests and second growth forests.


Behavior and ecology
The barred cuckoo-dove lives in small flocks.
It has a loud kro-uum or u-va vocalization, in which the second note is louder than the first.


Status and conservation
Since 1998, the barred cuckoo-dove has been listed least concern on the IUCN Red List, because it has a large range—more than 20,000 km2 (7,700 mi2) and the population trend is stable. Also, although its population numbers have not been determined, it is thought to comprise more than 10,000 individuals.


Local names
The Lepcha people of Sikkim call it ka ar fo.
The Mizo people of Mizoram call it Ṭhumimeisei.


References


External links
"Barred cuckoo dove" (Audio) (Recording). Xeno canto.
"Barred cuckoo-dove—Macropygia unchall". Encyclopedia of Life.
Oriental Bird Images: Barred Cuckoo Dove  Selected images

The '''barred cuckoo-dove''' ('''''Macropygia unchall''''') is a [[bird]] species in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is native to [[South Asia|South]] and [[Southeast Asia]], and listed as [[Least Concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

== Taxonomy ==
[[File:MacropygiaUnchall.jpg|thumb|Illustration of male and female<ref>{{cite book |title=Indian pigeons and doves |year=1913 |author=Stuart Baker, E. C. |publisher=Witherby & Co. |location=London |chapter=The Bar-tailed Cuckoo-dove |pages=238–243 |chapter-url=https://archive.org/details/indianpigeonsdov00bake/page/238}}</ref>]]
German [[Herpetology|herpetologist]] [[Johann Georg Wagler]] first described the barred cuckoo-dove in 1827. It has three recognized subspecies:<ref name=HBWA2017>{{cite book |chapter=Barred Cuckoo-dove (''Macropygia unchall'') |chapter-url=http://www.hbw.com/species/barred-cuckoo-dove-macropygia-unchall |last1=Baptista|first1=L. F. |last2=Trail |first2=P. W. |year=2017 |editor1-last=del Hoyo |editor1-first=J. |editor2-last=Elliott |editor2-first=A. |title=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive |publisher=Lynx Edicions |location=Barcelona |access-date=2017-09-20  |last3=Horblit |first3=H. M. |last4=Boesman |first4=P. |doi=10.2173/bow.bacdov1.01 |editor3-last=Sargatal |editor3-first=J. |editor4-last=Christie |editor4-first=D. A. |editor5-last=de Juana |editor5-first=E.}}</ref>
* ''M. u. tusalia'' <small>(Blyth, 1843)</small>
* ''M. u. minor'' <small>(Swinhoe, 1870)</small>
* ''M. u. unchall'' <small>(Wagler, 1827)</small>

== Description ==
[[File:Barred Cuckoo-dove.jpg|thumb|Barred cuckoo-dove]]
The barred cuckoo-dove has a buff coloured throat and forehead which becomes pinkish grey at the [[Crown (anatomy)|crown]].<ref name=HBWA2017/> measures {{Convert|37|to|41|cm|abbr=on}} in length, and weighs {{Convert|153|to|182|g|abbr=on}}.  Its [[Iris (anatomy)|iris]] is yellow or pale brown, the [[beak]] is black and short, and the feet are red.<ref name=Brazil2009>{{Cite book |chapter=Pigeons and Doves III |chapter-url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7-GgBoivL70C&pg=PA248 |last1=Brazil|first1=M. |title=Birds of East Asia. Eastern China, Taiwan, Korea, Japan and Eastern Russia |location=London |year=2009 |publisher=Christopher Helm |isbn=978-0-7136-7040-0|page=248}}</ref><ref name=MacKinnon2000>{{Cite book |chapter=Barred Cuckoo Dove ''Macropygia unchall'' |chapter-url=https://books.google.com/books?id=ZaXi-P-heDQC&pg=PA133 |title=A Field Guide to the Birds of China |last1=MacKinnon |first1=J. R. |last2=Phillipps |first2=K. |last3=He |first3=F. |year=2000 |publisher=[[Oxford University Press]] |location=Oxford |isbn=978-0-19-854940-6 |pages=[https://archive.org/details/fieldguidetobird00john/page/133 133] |url-access=registration |url=https://archive.org/details/fieldguidetobird00john/page/133 }}</ref> It has blackish brown [[upperparts]]. The back, [[Glossary of bird terms#mantle|mantle]] (between the [[nape]] and the starting of the back), rump, wing coverts, and scapulars have reddish brown fringes. The tail is blackish brown, and is heavily barred reddish brown.<ref name=Brazil2009/><ref name=Grewal2017>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=mzupDQAAQBAJ&pg=PA224 |page=224 |title=A Photographic Field Guide to the Birds of India, Pakistan, Nepal, Bhutan, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh |first1=B. |last1=Grewal |first2=S. |last2=Sen |first3=S. |last3=Singh |first4=N. |last4=Devasar |first5=G. |last5=Bhatia |type=Paperback |year=2017 |location=Princeton, New Jersey |isbn=978-0-691-17649-9 |publisher=[[Princeton University Press]]}}</ref>

It is similar to the [[little cuckoo-dove]], but it is much larger and darker, and is black-barred on the mantle, breast, coverts, and tail.<ref name=Gibbs2010>{{Cite book |title=Pigeons and Doves: A Guide to the Pigeons and Doves of the World |last1=Gibbs |first1=D. |author-link1=David Gibbs (naturalist)|year=2010 |publisher=A & C Black, [[Bloomsbury Publishing]] |isbn=978-1-4081-3555-6 |location=London }}</ref>

== Distribution and habitat ==
The barred cuckoo-dove occurs from the [[Himalayas]] to [[Southeast Asia]].<ref name=MacKinnon2000/> It inhabits dense subtropical woodlands at altitudes of {{Convert|800|to|3000|m|abbr=on}} from sea level, on [[Montane ecosystems|montane]] slopes.<ref name=Brazil2009/><ref name=MacKinnon2000/> It prefers clearings and edges of [[old-growth forest]]s and [[Secondary forest|second growth forests]].<ref name=Gibbs2010/><ref name=Grewal2017/>

== Behavior and ecology ==
The barred cuckoo-dove lives in small flocks.<ref name=Brazil2009/>
It has a loud ''kro-uum'' or ''u-va'' vocalization, in which the second note is louder than the first.<ref name=MacKinnon2000/>

== Status and conservation ==
Since 1998, the barred cuckoo-dove has been listed [[least concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List]], because it has a large range—more than 20,000 km<sup>2</sup> (7,700 mi<sup>2</sup>) and the population trend is stable. Also, although its population numbers have not been determined, it is thought to comprise more than 10,000 individuals.<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

== Local names ==
The [[Lepcha people]] of [[Sikkim]] call it {{lang|lep|ka ar fo}}.<ref>{{cite book |last1=Tamsang |first1=K. P. |title=The Lepcha-English Encyclopaedic Dictionary |date=1980 |publisher=Mrs. Mayel Clymit Tamsang |location=Kalimpong |page=121}}</ref>
The [[Mizo people]] of [[Mizoram]] call it Ṭhumimeisei.

==References==

zh.extract:
斑尾鹃鸠（学名：Macropygia unchall）为鸠鸽科鹃鸠属的鸟类。分布于自克什米尔地区、至印度、尼泊尔、不丹、孟加拉、缅甸、中南半岛、马来半岛、印度尼西亚以及中国大陆的华南一带北抵四川、福建等地，主要生活于丘陵的树林中。该物种的模式产地在印度尼西亚爪哇。


描述

翅膀和尾巴是深褐色，身體的其他部位更接近棕褐色。雄性胸部有大量的橫斑條紋，且雌雄皆有淺綠色的頸背。 體長37至41 cm（15至16英寸），重153至182 g（5.4至6.4 oz）。虹膜呈黃色或淺棕色，喙短，顏色為黑色，腳呈紅色。
與棕頭鵑鳩（little cuckoo-dove）外型相似，但是體型更大，更黑，並且在上背、胸部、兩翼覆蓋處和尾巴上有大量深色條紋。


分佈和棲地
斑尾鹃鸠分佈範圍從喜馬拉雅山到東南亞。 棲息地為海拔800至3,000米（2,600至9,800英尺）的山地斜坡，其上生長的亞熱帶濃密森林。 此外更常出沒於舊林清除後的空地和邊緣，以及砍伐過次生林。


習性
以一小群的方式活動及覓食。生性害羞，接近時會迅速逃離，可以很快的速度在茂密的林木中飛行，在野外很難觀察到。在飛行中和地面上觀察到時，其尾巴都會顯得比斑鳩更長。


保护
中国国家重点保护动物等级：二级


亚种
斑尾鹃鸠华南亚种（学名：Macropygia unchall minor）（Swinhoe, 1870）。在中国大陆，分布于福建、广东、海南、上海等地。该物种的模式产地在海南。
斑尾鹃鸠西南亚种（学名：Macropygia unchall tusalia）（Blyth, 1843）。在中国大陆，分布于四川、云南等地。该物种的模式产地在印度大吉岭。
斑尾鹃鸠亚种（学名：Macropygia unchall unchall） （Wagler, 1827）
